herbalist.name: Herbalist
description: ## Herbalist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/22›
Vine leshys who study the physical and spiritual composition of their own bodies have discovered a strange herbal alchemy all their own.
::prereq{r="Vine Leshy"}

::ab[Herbalism (Su)]{l=1 icon=power ability="An herbalist's studies allow him a unique method of creating alchemical discoveries. He can attempt Profession (herbalist) checks in place of Craft (alchemy) checks for all purposes, and he gains a competence bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his alchemist level on Profession (herbalist) checks to create alchemical items and to forage for and process herbs. An herbalist uses his @HLWisdom score instead of his Intelligence score for all alchemist class abilities." alter="Alchemy~the alchemist's key ability score"}

::ab[Natural Magic]{l=1 icon=learn passive="An herbalist's study of the leshy spirit grants him access to natural magic that eludes other alchemists. Each time he gains access to a new level of extracts (including 1st level), he can add two spells from that spell level on the ‹main/druid spell« list› to his alchemist class spell list; these spells must target a creature or creatures or have a range of &quot&you,&quot& and he must still add them to his formula book as normal before he can prepare them." special="As always, the extracts affect the drinker as if she were the target, so offensive touch spells are a nasty trap for the drinker." replace="Throw Anything"}

::ab[Seedpods (Su)]{icon=zap ability="An herbalist can produce and throw seedpods as ranged touch attacks, similar to the ranged attacks of other leshys. He can create a number of seedpods each day equal to his alchemist level + his Wisdom modifier and can throw them as a standard action with a range increment of 20 feet. A seedpod deals 1d6 points of bludgeoning damage, plus an additional 1d6 points of damage at 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter (this additional damage isn't multiplied on a critical hit or from effects like ‹feat/Vital Strike›). Seedpods are considered weapons and can be selected for feats like ‹feat/Weapon Focus›, and the herbalist can take the ‹feat/Extra Bombs› feat to gain additional daily uses of his seedpods. His seedpods also gain one of the following additional effects unless the target succeeds at a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + half the herbalist's alchemist level + his Wisdom modifier)." imp1="His seedpods can ‹misc/deafen› their target for 1 round like a ‹monster/leaf leshy›." imp2="They can reduce the target's vision to 10 feet for 1 minute (unless the target spends a full-round action to wash the spores from its eyes early) like the spores of a ‹monster/fungus leshy›." imp5="They can ‹rule/entangle<d› their target for 2d4 rounds (unless the target succeeds at a Strength check or Escape Artist check at the save DC to break the vine early) like the seeds of a ‹monster/gourd leshy›." imp8="They can ‹misc/sicken› their target for 1d4 rounds like the spittle of a ‹monster/flytrap leshy›." imp10="They can ‹misc/blind› their target until the end of their next turn like the water jets of a ‹monster/seaweed leshy›." replace="Bombs~poison use~poison resistance~poison immunity"}

homunculist.name: Homunculist
description: ## Homunculist
‹SOURCE Familiar Folio/8›
One of the grand goals of alchemy is the ability to create new life. A homunculist has made this dream a reality, growing and modifying a familiar in his own laboratory.
::ab[Diminished Poisoning]{icon=down passive="A homunculist doesn't gain the *poison use, poison resistance, poison immunity,* or *swift poisoning* class features."}

::ab[Homunculus Familiar (Ex)]{icon=power ability="The homunculist has created a living ‹monster/homunculus› in the shape of an animal or vermin. It functions in all ways as a ‹ability/familiar›, treating the homunculist's alchemist level as his effective wizard level." replace=Mutagen}

::ab[Experimentation (Ex)]{icon=power l=4 ability="A homunculist is constantly experimenting on his ‹sidekick/familiar› to give it new and unusual features and abilities. The homunculist can grant his familiar 1 evolution point worth of eidolon ‹ability/evolutions› for every 4 alchemist levels he possesses, though he can't select any evolutions that require a particular base form. Each time the homunculist gains a level, he can change his familiar's evolutions. These evolutions stack with those from the ‹feat/Evolved Familiar› feat." order=ap passive="The homunculist can select the Evolved Familiar feat as an alchemist discovery if he meets its prerequisites."}

horticulturist.name: Horticulturist
description: ## Horticulturist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/35;Heroes of the Wild/24›
A horticulturist spends his time cultivating plants and plant creatures, allowing him to experiment with strange and wondrous herbal concoctions.

::ab[Seed Extract]{l=1 icon=learn passive="A horticulturist adds *summon nature's ally I-VI* as 1st- through 6th-level extracts, respectively, to his class extract list. For example, he automatically adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› to his formula book at 1st level and adds the next higher-level spell to his formula book each time he gains the ability to use extracts of that level. The horticulturist uses these special extracts to plant a seed that grows into a creature, bypassing the restriction for single target or personal effects. Using these extracts takes 1 round (as the spell's normal casting time), and the creature summoned must be placed in a space adjacent to the horticulturist. The horticulturist can use these extracts to summon only animals and plants on the appropriate summon nature's ally list." special="Creatures the horticulturist summons with these extracts become creatures with the plant type (gaining low-light vision and immunity to mind-affecting, paralysis, poison, polymorph, sleep, and stunning effects) rather than their normal type, but their attack bonuses, Hit Dice, saving throws, and skills don't change. A horticulturist can't choose mutagen discoveries." replace="Mutagen~2nd-level discovery"}

::ab[Herbal Bombs (Su)]{l=1 icon=down-boost ability="A horticulturist's bombs lack the power of a traditional alchemist's but can be fine-tuned in ways normal alchemist explosives cannot. The horticulturist's bombs deal 1d4 points of damage (instead of 1d6), plus 1d4 points of damage for every 2 alchemist levels beyond 1st. When the horticulturist creates a bomb, he can formulate it to affect plants and other creatures normally, affect only plant creatures, or affect only creatures not of the plant type." alter=Bombs}

::ab[Plant Voice (Ex, Su)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="A horticulturist gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Survival checks." imp10="He can speak to plants as a constant ‹spell/speak with plants› spell (this ability is supernatural)." replace="Poison resistance~poison use~poison immunity"}

::ab[Plant Familiar (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A horticulturist creates a Diminutive or Tiny alchemical plant creature that vaguely resembles a kind of animal suitable for a familiar (such as a cat) and acts as his ‹ability/familiar›, granting the horticulturist a skill bonus, the ‹feat/Alertness› feat, and so on. The plant familiar has all the abilities of the animal it resembles (for example, a bat-like plant familiar can fly) but is treated as a creature of the plant type and has familiar abilities based on the horticulturist's alchemist level (though some familiar abilities may be useless to the horticulturist)." special="An alchemist's extracts and mutagens are considered spells for the purposes of familiar abilities like *share spells* or *deliver touch spells.* If a plant familiar is lost or dies, it can be replaced 1 week later through a specialized procedure that costs 200 gp per alchemist level the horticulturist has. The ritual takes 8 hours to complete." replace="The discovery gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Fury of Nature (Sp)]{l=14 icon=magic ability="A horticulturist's connection with nature allows him to bring down nature's fury on his opponents. He can cast one of the following spells, chosen at the time of casting, once per day as a spell-like ability: ‹spell/call lightning storm›, ‹spell/eagle aerie›, or ‹spell/liveoak›." replace="Persistent mutagen"}

ice_chemist.name: Ice Chemist
description: ## Ice Chemist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/35›
Reclusive alchemists who dwell in the highest mountains or on the frozen tundra devote their skills to mastering the cold.

::ab[Icy Bombs (Su)]{l=1 icon=power ability="An ice chemist's bombs deal cold damage instead of fire damage. In place of an alchemist discovery, she can learn to create bombs that deal fire damage like normal alchemist bombs do, though she can't apply discoveries that modify bombs to such fire bombs that she creates. An ice alchemist's icy bombs can still be poisonous or create thick vapors similar to smoke, but she cannot apply the ‹discovery/explosive<_bomb› or ‹discovery/inferno bomb› discoveries to them." alter=Bombs}

::ab[Cold Sweat (Su)]{l=2 flavor="An ice chemist channels her affinity for cold energy into her personal space, keeping herself and her gear cool." icon=def passive="She gains cold ‹umr/resistance› equal to her alchemist level and can survive comfortably in temperatures as low as -50&deg& Fahrenheit." replace="Poison resistance~poison immunity"}
::ab[Frost Bomb (Su)]{l=2 icon=power ability="An ice chemist gains the ‹discovery/frost bomb› discovery." replace="2nd-level discovery"}

inspired_chemist.name: Inspired Chemist
description: ## Inspired Chemist
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/75›

Akin to a mindchemist, inspired chemists use a type of cognatogen that instead of increasing their mental ability scores grants them inspiration like an investigator. This inspiration also grants an inspired chemist amazing powers to avoid danger, but takes a toll on both physical power and health.

::ab[Inspiring Cognatogen]{l=1 icon=power ability="An inspired chemist learns how to create an inspiring cognatogen, as the ‹discovery/inspiring cognatogen› discovery." replace=Mutagen special="An inspired chemist can learn to create mutagens if he selects the ‹discovery/mutagen› discovery."}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power passive="An inspired chemist can select ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Disable Device, Disguise, Heal, any Knowledge skill, Sense Motive, Spellcraft, or Use Magic Device) in place of a discovery."}

::ab[Bonus Investigator Talents]{icon=power passive="An inspired chemist can select any two ‹ability/investigator talents› in place of a discovery, but can only use these talents while under the effect of an inspiring cognatogen."}
::ab[Languages]{icon=power passive="An inspired chemist can learn three ‹misc/languages› in place of a discovery."}
@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the inspired chemist archetype: ‹discovery/grand inspiring cognatogen›, ‹discovery/greater inspiring cognatogen›, and ‹discovery/inspired bomb›.

internal_alchemist.name: Internal Alchemist
description: ## Internal Alchemist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/18›

An internal alchemist studies medicine, diet, and the living body to purify the self in the hope of gaining immortality by means of alchemical concoctions and controlling vital energy. Internal alchemists develop unusual physical abilities from heightened knowledge of how their bodies work. An internal alchemist has the following class features.

::ab[Breath Mastery]{l=1 icon=power-def-magic flavor="The internal alchemist can control his breath and the flow of vital energy within his body." ability="Without preparation, he can ‹misc/hold his breath› for a number of minutes equal to his Constitution score (after this, he must begin making Constitution checks or risk suffocation)." fullround="By spending a @HLfull-round_action preparing himself, he can increase this duration to 1 hour per point of Constitution." move="He can put himself into a state of suspended animation as a @HLmove_action, and is then unconscious and appears completely dead; he awakens at a preset time or in response to a condition set by him when he enters this state." passive="The alchemist can survive twice as long as normal without food or water before he starts to take penalties." order=arpm replace="Throw Anything"}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power passive="An internal alchemist can select ‹feat/Alertness›, ‹feat/Extra Ki›, ‹feat/Great Fortitude›, ‹feat/Improved Critical› (unarmed strike), ‹feat/Improved Initiative›, ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike›, ‹feat/Iron Will›, ‹feat/Lightning Reflexes›, ‹feat/Stunning Fist›, or ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (unarmed strike) in place of an alchemical discovery."}

::ab[Disease Resistance]{l=3 icon=def passive="An internal alchemist gains a bonus on all saving throws against disease equal to his alchemist class bonus against poison." special="When he gains the *poison immunity* alchemist class ability, he becomes immune to disease as well." replace="Swift alchemy"}

::ab[Uncanny Dodge]{l=6 flavor="An internal alchemist can react to danger before his senses would normally allow him to do so." icon=def passive="He cannot be caught ‹rule/flat-footed›, nor does he lose his Dexterity bonus to AC if the attacker is invisible. However, he still loses his Dexterity bonus to AC if he is immobilized, or if an opponent successfully uses the feint action (see ‹skill/Bluff›) against him." special="If the internal alchemist already has *uncanny dodge* from a different class, he automatically gains ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge› (as a rogue) instead." replace="Swift poisoning"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the internal alchemist archetype: ‹discovery/cognatogen›, ‹discovery/elixir of life›, ‹discovery/grand cognatogen›, ‹discovery/greater cognatogen›, ‹discovery/lingering spirit›, ‹discovery/mummification›, and ‹discovery/spontaneous healing›.

interrogator.name: Interrogator
description: ## Interrogator
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/18›
An interrogator extracts vital information from reluctant individuals using alchemical and traditional methods.
::cskill{gain=intm~sm}

::ab[Injections (Su)]{l=1 icon=power usage="An interrogator uses his skill with alchemy to create injections. Each day, an interrogator can create a number of injections equal to his alchemist level + his Intelligence modifier. It takes 1 hour to create the injections. An injection is inert until the interrogator attempts to use it." icon=touch standard="Using an injection is a standard action that requires a melee touch attack. If the attack hits, the target of an injection takes a -2 penalty on its Will saving throws for a number of minutes equal to half the interrogator's class level (minimum 1). If the interrogator is also using a *serum* with this injection, the penalty on Will saves applies to any save against the *serum.* If the melee touch attack fails, the injection isn't delivered but can still be used again - once activated, it remains potent for 1 day." special="An injection that hasn't been administered becomes inert if it leaves the interrogator's possession, reactivating as soon as it returns to his possession. The penalties from multiple injections don't stack, nor do the penalties from injections stack with penalties from a ‹class/mesmerist«'s› stare or a witch's ‹hex/evil eye› hex. Injections are mind-affecting poison effects." replace=Bomb}

::ab[Serums (Su)]{l=1 head icon=power order=wa flavor="An interrogator augments his ability to influence others through supplemental serums." swift="An interrogator prepares his serums at the same time as his injections and he can apply them to any injection as a @HLswift_action before he makes the attack roll for the injection." ability="An interrogator knows one serum at 1st level and can learn another serum in place of a discovery. A creature can be under the effect of only a single serum at a time - with the exception of *memory modification* serum - with any further serums on the same creature replacing the old serum." special="The save DCs of serums are equal to 10 + ‹misc/half› the interrogator's alchemist level + his Intelligence modifier. Unless otherwise noted, a creature that succeeds on its saving throw against a serum is immune to that serum's effects for 24 hours. An interrogator can never gain any sort of mutagen or cognatogen, even from a discovery or another class." replace="Mutagen~persistent mutagen"}

::ab[Charming]{sub icon=magic ability="The injected creature must succeed at a Will save or treat the interrogator favorably, as if affected by ‹spell/charm person› with a caster level equal to the interrogator's alchemist level." imp8="This serum acts as ‹spell/charm monster›."}

::ab[Compliance]{sub icon=magic prereq="8th-level interrogator" passive="On a failed Will save, the subject of this injection is affected by a ‹spell/suggestion› with a caster level equal to the interrogator's alchemist level."}
::ab[Confusion]{sub icon=lower prereq="6th-level interrogator" passive="On a failed Will save, the injected creature is ‹misc/confused› for 1 round per alchemist level the interrogator has."}
::ab[Hypnosis]{sub icon=lower passive="The injected creature must succeed at a Will save or become ‹misc/fascinated› for 1 round per alchemist level the interrogator has."}

::ab[Memory Modification]{sub icon=magic prereq="12th-level interrogator" passive="On a failed Will save, the target of this serum is affected by ‹spell/modify memory› at a caster level equal to the interrogator's alchemist level."}

::ab[Paralytic]{sub icon=magic prereq="8th-level interrogator" passive="On a failed Will save, the injected creature is affected by ‹spell/hold person› with a caster level equal to the interrogator's alchemist level."}

::ab[Truth]{sub icon=magic passive="A creature injected with a truth serum must succeed at a Will save or be compelled to answer the interrogator's questions truthfully. The interrogator can ask one question per 2 alchemist levels (minimum 1). Answers might be brief, cryptic, or repetitive, especially if the injected creature is unfriendly, but they aren't false. If a creature has been subjected to a truth serum within the past week, whether it succeeded at or failed its save, any new serum fails."}

mad_scientist.name: Mad Scientist
description: ## Mad Scientist
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/45›

The mad scientist's colleagues laughed at her insights, mocked her theories, and hounded her from the halls of academia, simply because they were too weak and frightened to understand her work. They thought she was finished. They were wrong.

::ab[Mad Genius (Su)]{l=2 icon=magic-down ability="A mad scientist can deal 1d3 points of ‹rule/Wisdom damage› to herself (or 1d6 points of sanity damage if employing the ‹rule/sanity« rules›) to create a genius extract as one of her daily 1st-, 2nd-, 3rd-, 4th-, or 5th-level extracts. This otherwise takes the same amount of time and effort as creating a normal extract of its level, but instead of her choosing a formula she knows for it to grant, a genius extract has an unpredictable result. When the alchemist (or an ally, if she has the ‹discovery/infusion› discovery) drinks the extract, it produces a random effect from the list of alchemist formulae of 1 spell level higher than the extract's spell level. For instance, if she creates a genius extract using a 2nd-level extract, it would produce the effect of a random 3rd-level alchemist formula." order=ap passive="There is no way to predict which effect a genius extract will produce before drinking it. A genius extract's minimum caster level is equal to the minimum caster level of the extract used, not the effect produced. So, for instance, a genius extract that produced a ‹spell/fox's cunning› effect would have a minimum caster level of 1 (for the 1st-level extract used) rather than 4 (for the 2nd-level effect)." special="There are 111 potential 2nd-level extracts, 89 potential 3rd-level extracts, 63 potential 4th-level extracts, 39 potential 5th-level extracts, and 33 potential 6th-level extracts." alter=Alchemy replace="2nd-level discovery"}

::ab[Mad Mutagen (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost-down ability="A mad scientist can brew a mad mutagen in place of a normal mutagen. If she does so, she need not make any decisions about the mutagen (including whether to make it a ‹discovery/cognatogen› or another variant mutagen if she has the appropriate discoveries) when she brews it. When the mad scientist drinks her mad mutagen, she makes those decisions on the fly, but she also takes 1d4 points of Wisdom damage (or 1d8 points of sanity damage if using the sanity rules). If anyone else, even another alchemist, drinks a mad mutagen, that person must succeed at a Will save (with the same DC as for the Fortitude save against nausea) or she becomes ‹misc/confused› for 1 hour." alter=Mutagen replace="4th-level discovery"}

metamorph.name: Metamorph
description: ## Metamorph
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/19›
Metamorphs eschew traditional alchemy, focusing on internal chemistry and the transfiguration of living forms.
::cskill{gain=dis}

::ab[Shapechanger (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-magic standard="At 1st level, a metamorph gains the ability to transform herself into another form. This ability functions like ‹spell/alter self›, except as noted here. The effect lasts for 1 hour per metamorph's alchemist level, or until she changes back. Changing form (to another form or back) is a standard action that doesn't provoke attacks of opportunity. The metamorph is ‹misc/fatigued› for 1 round each time she changes shape." provokes=No special="A metamorph can use this ability once per day at 1st level, and an additional time per day at 4th level, and every 2 alchemist levels thereafter, for a total of nine times at 18th level." imp20="A metamorph can use *shapechanger* as a @HLfree_action once per round, either before or after all her other actions, with no daily limit." imp5="This ability can also function as ‹spell/monstrous physique I›." imp9="This ability can also function as ‹spell/monstrous physique II›." imp11="This ability can also function as ‹spell/monstrous physique III›." imp13="This ability can also function as ‹spell/monstrous physique IV› or ‹spell/giant form I›." imp15="This ability can also function as ‹spell/giant form II›." replace="Alchemy~bombs~extracts~mutagen~Throw Anything"}

::ab[Mutagen (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A metamorph gains *mutagen* as a normal alchemist. She can take *mutagen* and *cognatogen* discoveries despite not possessing the alchemy class feature that normally grants mutagen."}

::ab[Adaptive Physiology (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A metamorph gains a 25% chance to negate critical hits and ‹misc/precision damage›, similar to the ‹discovery/preserve organs› discovery." imp6="This chance becomes 50%." imp18="This chance increases to 75%." replace="Swift alchemy~swift poisoning~instant alchemy"}

mindchemist.name: Mindchemist
description: ## Mindchemist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/19›

While most alchemists use mutagens to boost their physical ability at the cost of mental ability, some use alchemy for the opposite purpose - to boost the power of the mind and memory. A mindchemist can reach incredible levels of mental acuity, but suffers lingering debilitating effects to his physique. A mindchemist has the following class features.

::ab[Cognatogen]{l=1 icon=power ability="A mindchemist learns how to create a cognatogen, as per the ‹discovery/cognatogen› discovery. (A mindchemist cannot create mutagens unless he selects ‹discovery/mutagen› as a discovery.)" replace=Mutagen}

::ab[Perfect Recall]{l=2 icon=boost passive="When making a Knowledge check, he may add his Intelligence bonus on the check a second time. Thus, a mindchemist with 5 ranks in Knowledge (history) and a +2 Intelligence bonus has a total skill bonus of +9 (5 + 2 + 2) using this ability. The mindchemist can also use this ability when making an Intelligence check to remember something." replace="Poison use"}
::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power passive="A mindchemist may select ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Disable Device, Disguise, Heal, any Knowledge skill, Sense Motive, or Spellcraft) in place of a discovery."}
::ab[Languages]{icon=power passive="A mindchemist may learn three ‹misc/languages› in place of a discovery."}
@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the mindchemist archetype: ‹discovery/grand cognatogen›, ‹discovery/greater cognatogen›, ‹discovery/infuse mutagen›.

mixologist.name: Mixologist
description: ## Mixologist
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Taverns/57›
Certain alchemists focus on mastering the chemical reactions between alcohol and the physical body. Though the powerful potions they brew can have drawbacks, they are at least pleasurable.
@HL[Note:] This archetype makes heavy use of the alternate ‹rule/drunkenness› rules.

::ab[Alcoholic Alchemy (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-boost flavor="A mixologist can infuse potions and extracts he creates with alcohol, increasing their potency." ability="When a mixologist brews a potion, he can spend an additional 10 gp in raw materials to increase his caster level by 1 for the purpose of the potion's effects, but the potion counts as a **strong alcoholic drink.** When a mixologist creates his extracts, he can spend 1 extra minute doing so to increase his caster level by 1 for the purpose of that extract's effects, but the extract counts as a **strong alcoholic drink.** A mixologist can prepare one alcoholic extract for each level of extracts he can prepare." special="A mixologist can't ever gain a mutagen or discoveries that alter mutagens." alter="Alchemy~brew potion" replace=Mutagen}

::ab[Alcoholic Bombs (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost ability="When the mixologist creates a bomb, he can cause the detonation to produce intoxicating fumes. A creature that takes a direct hit from an alcoholic bomb becomes @HLtipsy if it fails a Fortitude saving throw. If the creature is already tipsy, it becomes @HLdrunk instead. If the creature is already drunk, it becomes @HLsoused instead. A soused creature that fails its Fortitude saving throw passes out for 1d3 hours and wakes up with a @HLhangover." special="This ability counts as a discovery that modifies bombs (Primary Bomb Discovery), so it doesn't stack with other discoveries that modify bombs." replace="The discovery gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Alcoholic Resistance (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A mixologist's bonus on saving throws against poison also applies on saving throws to become less drunk and to avoid passing out. This doesn't allow a mixologist to become immune to alcohol." alter="Poison resistance"}

::ab[Mixologist Master (Su)]{l=14 icon=boost ability="A mixologist can have up to two alcoholic extracts for each level of extracts he can prepare as long as he can prepare at least two extracts of that level." replace="Persistent mutagen"}

mnemostiller.name: Mnemostiller
description: ## Mnemostiller
‹SOURCE Distant Realms/52›

Mnemostillers extract memories, storing them as a thick vapor called mnemos. They can return memories as easily as administering a potion, and experienced mnemostillers create a variety of effects through the careful administration of past traumas or victories.

::ab[Mental Ambix]{l=1 icon=power-magic flavor="A mnemostiller's alchemy relies on force of personality, using his own mind as an alchemy lab to draw and purify the mnemos collected from others." passive="A mnemostiller uses his Charisma score in place of his Intelligence score for the purpose of extracts and bonus extracts. A mnemostiller still uses his Intelligence score to learn new formulae." order=ps standard="The mnemostiller can cast ‹spell/detect thoughts› as a spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to his Charisma modifier (minimum 1)." alter=Alchemy replace="Throw anything"}

::ab[Rasugen (Su)]{l=1 icon=def-boost-down flavor="A mnemostiller learns to brew a unique type of mutagen, called a rasugen, that suppresses his mind to enter a state of sublime purity. In this state, a mnemostiller becomes incredibly resilient, but cannot perform complex skills." ability="Once imbibed, a rasugen grants a +2 alchemical bonus on all saving throws and 2 ‹rule/temporary hit points› per alchemist level for 10 minutes per alchemist level." special="In addition, while the rasugen is in effect, a mnemostiller takes a -2 penalty to his Intelligence score and can't attempt checks using Appraise, Craft, Disable Device, Heal, Knowledge (any), Profession, Sleight of Hand, or Spellcraft. This acts in all other ways like a mutagen. A mnemostiller can never gain the *mutagen, cognatogen,* or *inspiring cognatogen* ability, even from a discovery or another class." replace=Mutagen}

::ab[Anguish Bomb (Su)]{l=1 icon=power flavor="A mnemostiller crafts his bombs from mnemos of pain and discomfort, inflicting psychic damage." ability="An anguish bomb is identical to a bomb except that it deals nonlethal damage, it deals no damage to inanimate objects and creatures immune to mental effects, and creatures caught in the anguish bomb's splash can attempt a Will save for half damage. A mnemostiller uses his Charisma score in place of his Intelligence score to determine the bonus damage of his anguish bomb and the save DC of the anguish bomb's splash damage. He still uses his Intelligence score to determine his number of bombs per day." special="If the mnemostiller has taken any hit point damage in the past 24 hours, his anguish bombs inflict 1 additional point of nonlethal damage for every 2 alchemist levels he has." replace=Bombs}

::ab[Brewed Memories]{l=1 flavor="A mnemostiller can create several unique extracts from the mnemos he pulls from the minds of others." icon=learn passive="He adds ‹spell/mindlink›, ‹spell/placebo effect›, and ‹spell/thought echo› to his formula book as 1st-level extracts." imp5="He adds ‹spell/aura alteration›, ‹spell/detect mindscape›, and ‹spell/mindscape door› to his formula book as 2nd-level extracts." imp8="He adds ‹spell/mindwipe› and ‹spell/thoughtsense› to his formula book as 3rd-level extracts; the range for a *mindwipe* extract becomes creature touched, and it can be applied only to a helpless or willing creature." replace="Poison resistance"}
::ab[Natural Empath (Ex)]{l=2 flavor="A mnemostiller's studies naturally focus on others." icon=power ability="He gains ‹discovery/infusion› as a bonus discovery at 2nd level." replace="Poison use"}
::ab[Mind-Delver (Su)]{l=10 icon=power-magic ability="A mnemostiller can use ‹spell/mind probe› as a spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to his Charisma modifier (minimum 1)."}
### Mnemostil

First developed to help heal souls who had experienced undeath for a time, the art of mnemostil involves a practitioner carefully extracting or returning memories as an oily vapor - mnemos - which allows tortured souls to confront their past horrors one at a time while finding closure. In a city largely dedicated to waiting out eternity and cultivating absent passion, however, there are abundant souls eager to feel anything, good or bad. Black-market mnemostillers pay handsomely to extract memories from remarkable souls, even traveling to other planes to build up their stock before returning to Spire's Edge to sell their wares. While there is some crossover with information brokers, mnemostillers typically sell experiences and emotions rather than secrets. Petitioners searching for purpose buy what memories they can to try and shape themselves toward one Outer Plane destination or another, often trading in their genuine memories as payment, until little of the original individual remains. Some criminal syndicates target visiting souls and outsiders to extract their memories by force.

Mnemostil has also created a unique faith in Spire's Edge: amnesia cults. Many petitioners succumb to the expectation that they grow - that who they were in life is somehow not enough - and so render themselves into temporary or permanent blank slates to dwell in inert bliss. Others cannot reconcile their own actions and so wipe themselves clean to live in ignorance.

Pruning the development of the soul is anathema to the purpose of Spire's Edge, and the city's ‹family/psychopomp«s› aggressively hunt both mnemostillers and amnesia cultists, with punishment for the crime invariably being exile into the Astral Plane.

oenopion_researcher.name: Oenopion Researcher
description: ## Oenopion Researcher
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Magic/37›

Nex is known for its advanced magical lore, and the alchemists of Oenopion are incredibly skilled at making potions, elixirs, and other materials crucial to the country's economy and its monster-creating fleshforges. A Oenopion researcher has the following class features.

::ab[Experimental Mutagen (Su)]{l=2 icon=power ability="An Oenopion researcher learns how to create an experimental mutagen that benefits others, though not as well as his normal mutagen. The experimental mutagen works just like a standard mutagen, except the natural armor bonus and the alchemical bonus to the ability score are half normal (+1 natural armor bonus and +2 to one ability score). The experimental mutagen has no risk of nauseating a creature who drinks it. The researcher decides when he creates the mutagen if it is a standard mutagen (which gives no benefit if another creature drinks it) or an experimental mutagen (which does); there is no difference in the cost, time to create, or any other aspect of the mutagen." special="If the researcher has discoveries or other abilities that alter or increase the benefits of the mutagen, these apply to the experimental mutagen (though the drinker only gets ‹misc/half› the numerical bonus of the mutagen)." replace="Poison use"}
::ab[Acid Resistance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="An Oenopion researcher gains acid resistance 5." replace="Swift alchemy"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the Oenopion researcher archetype: ‹discovery/acid bomb›, ‹discovery/bottled ooze›, ‹discovery/feral mutagen›, ‹discovery/infuse mutagen›, ‹discovery/infusion›, ‹discovery/sunlight bomb›.

oozemaster.name: Oozemaster
description: ## Oozemaster
‹SOURCE Monster Hunter's Handbook/17›

The oozemaster is a specialized alchemist who fights and ensnares oozes. An oozemaster carefully studies the characteristics of the oozes he hunts to help him create new and more effective mutagens and bombs.
::cskill{gain=kd lose=kna}

::ab[Ooze Bomb (Su)]{icon=power ability="An oozemaster's bombs deal acid damage instead of fire damage, and they deal damage only on a direct hit; they do not splash. A creature damaged by an ooze bomb takes additional acid damage equal to the oozemaster's Intelligence modifier at the start of the oozemaster's next turn. A creature can take this delayed damage only once per round; additional hits extend the duration of the ongoing damage by 1 round each." special="An oozemaster cannot select discoveries that change the type of damage dealt by his bombs, nor those that affect the bomb's splash radius. An oozemaster can still select ‹feat/Brew Potion› without meeting the feat's prerequisites, but he doesn't gain it as a bonus feat." replace="Brew Potion"}

::ab[Ooze Resistance]{l=2 icon=def passive="He gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against the extraordinary and supernatural abilities of oozes." imp5="The bonus becomes +3." imp8="The bonus becomes +4." imp11="The bonus becomes +5." imp14="The bonus becomes +6." imp17="The bonus becomes +7." imp20="The bonus becomes +8." replace="Poison resistance~poison immunity"}
@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the oozemaster archetype: ‹discovery/bottled ooze›, ‹discovery/ooze blight›, and ‹discovery/splitting mutagen›.

perfumer.name: Perfumer
description: ## Perfumer
‹SOURCE Wilderness Origins/17›

While most alchemists focus on the flash of loud explosions, full-bodied potions, and dramatically transformative mutagens, others prefer subtlety and elegance. A perfumer alchemist refines extracts to their most pure and rarefied forms to spread on the wind, and would rather share a head-turning aroma at a social than drink a bone-twisting mutagen before a brawl.

::ab[Atomized Extracts (Su)]{icon=power-magic ability="A perfumer's extracts are stored in atomizers, rather than bottles or vials, allowing him to spray them at himself or an adjacent ally. The target is considered the imbiber, as if she had drunk an extract prepared with the ‹discovery/infusion› discovery. As the contents of an atomizer are inhaled, the target must be able to breathe. Atomized extracts are considered extracts for purposes of other abilities, and become inert when not in the alchemist's possession." alter=Extracts replace="Brew potion"}

::ab[Effervescent Bombs (Su)]{icon=power-zap ability="Rather than deal direct damage, a perfumer's bombs create an effervescent puddle in a 5-foot radius for a number of rounds equal to the alchemist's intelligence bonus (minimum 1). Each creature within this area takes 1d4 points of fire damage immediately and again each round it remains within or enters the puddle. A creature caught in the puddle when it is first created can attempt a Reflex save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the perfumer's level + the perfumer's Intelligence modifier) for half damage." special="The effervescent bombs' damage increases by 1d4 at 3rd level and every odd level thereafter. Effervescent bombs otherwise functions as bombs, and discoveries that apply to bombs apply to effervescent bombs." alter=Bomb}

::ab[Pheromones (Su)]{icon=power-boost-down order=as ability="A perfumer can spend 1 hour to distill a special mixture of exotic pheromones and alchemical reagents and store it in an atomizer. The mixture remains potent until used or until the perfumer distills another pheromone mixture, at which point the first becomes inert and the duration of its effects immediately end." standard="Spraying this mixture on a willing creature grants the target a +4 alchemical bonus to Charisma and a -2 penalty to Constitution for 10 minutes per alchemist level. Additionally, the target creature gains a +2 alchemical bonus on Diplomacy and Bluff checks." replace=Mutagen special="A perfumer can never gain the *mutagen, cognatogen,* or *inspired cognatogen* ability, even from a discovery or another class."}

::ab[Persistent Pheromones]{l=14 icon=boost ability="The alchemist gains the ability to distill extremely potent pheromones. His pheromone atomizers can be sprayed on up to four different targets before becoming fully used. This allows a perfumer to affect multiple targets with a single atomizer." replace="Persistent mutagen"}

plague_bringer.name: Plague Bringer
description: ## Plague Bringer
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/153›

The plague bringer sees disease as the ultimate weapon, and has worked tirelessly to master new diseases and disease-delivery systems. A plague bringer feels no more remorse at unleashing his armaments on his enemies than an archer does when firing an arrow in the heat of battle. Disease is a tool, and the plague bringer is its master. A plague bringer has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Ratfolk}

::ab[Plague Vial (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-melee flavor="A plague bringer can create a plague vial, an alchemically grown and concentrated disease sample." order=nsa info="It takes 1 hour to prepare a plague vial, and once prepared, the vial remains potent until used. A plague bringer can only maintain 1 plague vial at a time - if he prepares a second vial, any existing plague vial becomes inert. A plague vial that is not in a plague bringer's possession becomes inert until a plague bringer picks it up again." standard="A plague bringer can drink a plague vial. Anyone other than a plague bringer (including another alchemist) who drinks a plague vial must make a saving throw against the vial's DC or become ‹misc/nauseated› for 1 hour. Upon being imbibed, the plague vial infects the plague bringer's blood, sweat, tears, and other bodily fluids for 10 minutes per class level. Any creature that harms him with melee attacks (except with reach weapons) must make a Fortitude save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the plague bringer's level + the plague bringer's Intelligence modifier) or become ‹misc/sickened› for 1 round per plague bringer level." ability="As a @HLstandard_action, the plague bringer can infect a weapon with this sickness (typically by licking it or wiping his blood or pus on it). The disease on the weapon works like a poisoned weapon, except the source is a disease instead of a poison (so a dwarf's resistance to poison does not apply)." special="The plague bringer is immune to the effect of his own plague vial, but not that of another's plague vial. The effects of multiple plague vials do not stack. Unless he learns how to brew a mutagen by taking the ‹discovery/mutagen› discovery, he can never benefit from a mutagen and reacts to it as if he were a non-alchemist. At any particular time, a plague bringer can only be under the effect of either a plague vial or a mutagen (not both); drinking another immediately ends the effects of any ongoing plague vial or mutagen. All limitations to mutagens apply to plague vials as if they were the same substance. The ‹discovery/infuse mutagen› discovery and *persistent mutagen* class ability apply to plague vials. The ‹discovery/sticky poison› discovery applies to a weapon infected with a plague vial. The plague vial is a disease effect." replace=Mutagen}

::ab[Disease Resistance (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A plague bringer gains a +2 bonus on all saving throws against disease." imp5="This bonus increases to +4." imp8="This bonus increases to +6." imp10="A plague bringer becomes completely immune to disease (including magical diseases)." replace="Poison resistance~poison immunity"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the plague bringer archetype: ‹discovery/explosive bomb›, ‹discovery/precise bombs›, ‹discovery/smoke bomb›, ‹discovery/breath weapon bomb›, ‹discovery/fast bombs›, ‹discovery/nauseating flesh›; ‹discovery/plague bomb›.

preservationist.name: Preservationist
description: ## Preservationist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/19›

Some alchemists are obsessed with collecting and preserving exotic creatures. These preservationists may use bottled animals and monsters as teaching tools, but some learn how to reanimate them for short periods to battle on the alchemist's behalf. A preservationist has the following class features.

::ab[Bottled Ally I]{l=2 icon=skill-learn order=pa passive="A preservationist adds ‹skill/Handle Animal› (WIS) to his list of class skills." ability="He adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› to his formula book as a 1st-level extract. When he prepares that extract, he actually prepares a tiny, preserved specimen in a bottle (as with a caster casting the spell, the preservationist doesn't have to choose the creature until he uses the extract). When the alchemist opens the bottle, the specimen animates and grows to normal size, serving the preservationist as per the spell and otherwise being treated as a summoned creature. When the duration expires, the preserved creature decays into powder." special="If the preservationist has the ‹discovery/infusion› discovery, another character can use the infused specimen. The ‹feat/Augment Summoning› feat can be applied to these specimens." replace="Poison use"}
::ab[Bottled Ally II]{l=5 icon=learn passive="A preservationist adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally II› to his formula book as a 2nd-level extract." replace="Poison resistance +4"}
::ab[Bottled Ally III]{l=8 icon=learn passive="A preservationist adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally IV› to his formula book as a 3rd-level extract." replace="Poison resistance +6"}
::ab[Bottled Ally IV]{l=10 icon=learn passive="A preservationist adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally V› to his formula book as a 4th-level extract." replace="Poison immunity"}
::ab[Bottled Ally V]{l=14 icon=learn passive="A preservationist adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally VII› to his formula book as a 5th-level extract." replace="Persistent mutagen"}
::ab[Bottled Ally VI]{l=18 icon=learn passive="A preservationist adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally IX› to his formula book as a 6th-level extract." replace="18th-level discovery"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the preservationist archetype: ‹discovery/bottled ooze›, ‹discovery/preserve organs›, ‹discovery/tentacle›, ‹discovery/vestigial arm›, and ‹discovery/wings›.
@HL[Feats:] The ‹feat/Planar Preservationist› feat may be of particular interest to a Preservationist.

promethean_alchemist.name: Promethean Alchemist
description: ## Promethean Alchemist
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/112›
Obsessed with discovering the origins of life, promethean alchemists build constructs they gradually endow with life.
::ab[Craft Construct]{l=1 icon=power ability="A promethean alchemist gains the ‹discovery/promethean disciple› discovery." replace="Brew Potion~Throw Anything"}

::ab[Homunculus Companion (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A promethean alchemist gains the service of a homunculus companion. The means for creating this special homunculus are more exotic than normal and require investiture of the promethean alchemist's own life essence into the homunculus. This construct generally resembles a small humanoid or child when first created; the specific appearance is up to its creator, though a homunculus is always humanoid in shape and obviously unnatural on close inspection. While every homunculus companion is absolutely devoted to its master, some homunculi's loyalty borders on the unsettling; such companions jealously protect their masters from threats or construct strange shrines in their masters' honor. A promethean alchemist can't take the *mutagen* or *cognatogen* discoveries." special="A homunculus companion can be of any alignment, even one that is different from its master's. A destroyed homunculus companion can be restored to life by performing a ritual with its corpse that requires 1 pint of its creator's blood per Hit Die of the homunculus and rare materials worth 100 gp per Hit Die of the homunculus. Performing this ritual takes 1 hour (though it may take some time to gather enough blood at higher levels, so many promethean alchemists keep chilled blood samples in storage just in case)." replace=Bombs~mutagen}
@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement this archetype: ‹discovery/alchemical simulacrum›, ‹discovery/doppelganger simulacrum›, and ‹discovery/greater alchemical simulacrum›.
### Homunculus Companion Base Statistics
A homunculus companion has the following features.
| Level | HD | BAB | Saves | Skills | Feats | Str/Dex Bonus | Special |
| 1st | 1 | +1 | +0 | 2 | 1 | +0 | Darkvision, low-light vision, sympathetic alchemy, telepathic link, weapon and armor proficiency |
| 5th | 4 | +4 | +1 | 10 | 2 | +1 | Ability score increase |
| 9th | 7 | +7 | +2 | 16 | 4 | +3 | Spell resistance |
| 10th | 8 | +8 | +2 | 18 | 4 | +3 | Ability score increase |
| 15th | 12 | +12 | +4 | 26 | 6 | +5 | Ability score increase, improved evasion |
| 20th | 15 | +15 | +5 | 32 | 8 | +6 | Ability score increase |
@HL[Level] This is the homunculus's master's alchemist level.
@HL[HD] This is the homunculus's total number of 10-sided (d10) Hit Dice. The homunculus doesn't have a Constitution score, but it gains bonus hit points based on its size.
@HL[BAB] This is the base attack bonus of the homunculus, which is equal to its number of Hit Dice.
@HL[Saves] These are the base saving throw bonuses of the homunculus.

@HL[Skills] This entry lists the homunculus's total number of skill ranks. A homunculus with a high Intelligence score modifies these totals as normal (the homunculus gains a number of skill ranks equal to 2 + its Intelligence modifier per HD). A homunculus can't have more ranks in a skill than it has Hit Dice.

@HL[Homunculus Class Skills] The following skills are class skills for a homunculus: Bluff (Cha), Craft (Int), Fly (Dex), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Perception (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), and Stealth (Dex). In addition, at 1st level, a promethean alchemist can choose two additional skills to be class skills for his homunculus.
@HL[Feats] This is the total number of feats the homunculus has. A homunculus can select any feats it qualifies for, but must possess the appropriate appendages in order to use some feats.
@HL[Str/Dex Bonus] Add this modifier to the homunculus's Strength and Dexterity.
@HL[Special] As the homunculus increases in power, it gains the special abilities described below.
::ab[Darkvision (Ex)]{icon=power passive="The homunculus has darkvision out to a range of 60 feet."}
::ab[Low-Light Vision (Ex)]{icon=power passive="Homunculi see twice as far as humans in ‹rule/dim light›."}

::ab[Sympathetic Alchemy (Su)]{icon=boost-magic passive="The bond between a promethean alchemist and his homunculus is so close that the alchemist's extracts function for the homunculus as if it were the alchemist, allowing the homunculus companion to benefit from extracts without the alchemist needing the infusion discovery. The homunculus is treated as a humanoid or a construct - whichever is more beneficial - for the purposes of what extracts can affect it. Additionally, the homunculus can prepare its master's extracts from his formula book for him each day, as long as it's within the range of its telepathic link."}

::ab[Telepathic Link (Su)]{icon=power passive="A homunculus can't initially speak, but shares a telepathic link with its creator. It knows what its master knows and can convey to him everything it sees and hears, out to a range of 1,500 feet."}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency (Ex)]{icon=power passive="A homunculus is proficient with simple weapons, but not armor or shields."}
::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="If a homunculus is subjected to an attack that normally allows a Reflex save for half damage, it takes no damage if it succeeds at its saving throw."}
::ab[Ability Score Increase (Ex)]{icon=boost l=5 ability="The homunculus adds 1 to one of its ability scores at 5th level and every 5 levels thereafter."}
::ab[Speech (Ex)]{icon=power l=6 passive="The homunculus gains the ability to speak any languages it knows."}
::ab[Spell Resistance (Su)]{icon=def l=9 passive="The homunculus gains spell resistance equal to 11 + its master's alchemist level."}

::ab[Improved Evasion (Ex)]{l=15 icon=def passive="When subjected to an attack that allows a Reflex save for half damage, the homunculus takes no damage if it succeeds at the saving throw and only half damage if it fails the saving throw."}
### Homunculus Starting Statistics

**Size** Small; **Speed** 20 ft., fly 30 ft. (poor); **AC** +2 natural armor; **Attack** bite (1d4); **Ability Scores Str** 10, **Dex** 15, **Con** -, **Int** 10, **Wis** 12, **Cha** 7; **Special Qualities** darkvision, low-light vision; **Languages** Common (can't speak).
#### 4th-Level Advancement
**Size** Medium; **Speed** 30 ft., fly 40 ft. (average); **Attack** bite (1d6 plus poison), 2 claws (1d6); **Ability Scores Str** +4, **Dex** -2; **Special Attacks** poison.
::aff[Homunculus Poison]{iconP type=Bite-injury saveF=13 freqM=60 eff="sleep for 1 minute" cure1 extra="Con-based DC with a +2 racial bonus"}

psychonaut.name: Psychonaut
description: ## Psychonaut
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/19›
A psychonaut uses his knowledge to explore altered states of consciousness and even other planes of existence. A psychonaut has the following class features.

::ab[Bomb]{icon=down ability="A psychonaut's bombs deal damage ‹misc/one die step› lower than normal (regular bombs deal d4s, ‹discovery/concussive bomb«s› deal 1d3s, and so on). This ability otherwise functions as and replaces the standard alchemist bomb class feature." replace=Bombs}

::ab[Precognition]{l=5 icon=learn passive="A psychonaut adds ‹spell/augury› to his formula book as a 2nd-level extract (this extract does not require a divine focus component)." replace="Poison resistance +4"}

::ab[Psychic Senses]{l=8 icon=learn passive="A psychonaut adds ‹spell/clairaudience/clairvoyance›, ‹spell/detect scrying›, ‹spell/scrying›, and ‹spell/speak with dead› to his formula book as 3rd-level extracts (a *scrying* extract does not require a focus or divine focus component)." special="When a psychonaut uses a *speak with dead* extract, he gains the power to hear the lingering spirit within a corpse rather than enabling the corpse to audibly speak, but the extract otherwise works as the spell." replace="Poison resistance +6"}

::ab[Remote Consciousness]{l=10 icon=learn passive="A psychonaut adds ‹spell/dream›, ‹spell/lesser astral projection›, ‹spell/nightmare›, ‹spell/plane shift›, ‹spell/sending›, and ‹spell/telepathic bond› to his formula book as 4th-level extracts (a *plane shift* extract does not require a focus component)." replace="Poison immunity"}
::ab[Greater Precognition]{l=15 icon=learn passive="A psychonaut adds ‹spell/moment of prescience› to his formula book as a 5th-level extract." replace="15th-level bomb damage increase"}
::ab[Master Precognition]{l=17 icon=learn passive="A psychonaut adds ‹spell/foresight› to his formula book as a 6th-level extract." replace="17th-level bomb damage increase"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the psychonaut archetype: ‹discovery/confusion bomb›, ‹discovery/eternal potion›, ‹discovery/extend potion›, ‹discovery/lingering spirit›, and ‹discovery/madness bomb›.

ragechemist.name: Ragechemist
description: ## Ragechemist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/25›
Some alchemists create mutagens that tap into a primal anger that fuels their physical transformation. These alchemists have little control over their altered selves.

::ab[Rage Mutagen]{l=2 icon=power-boost-down ability="Whenever a ragechemist creates a mutagen that improves his Strength, that mutagen's bonus to Strength increases by +2 and penalizes the alchemist's Intelligence score." order=ap passive="While under the effects of this mutagen, whenever the alchemist takes damage, his rage grows, with detrimental effects. At the end of each turn that he takes hit point damage, the ragechemist must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC 15, or DC 20 if any of the damage came from a critical hit that turn) or take a -2 penalty on Will saving throws and to Intelligence. These penalties end 1 hour after the mutagen ends and stack with themselves." special="If the penalty lowers the ragechemist's Intelligence score to 0, the ragechemist is comatose until 1 hour after the mutagen expires." replace="Poison use"}

::ab[Sturdy Rage]{l=6 icon=def-down passive="Whenever a ragechemist uses his rage mutagen, he also gains a +4 bonus to natural armor, but the penalty on Will saving throws and to Intelligence for taking damage increases to -4." replace="Swift poisoning"}

::ab[Lumbering Rage]{l=10 icon=boost-down ability="Whenever a ragechemist uses his rage mutagen ability, he may have the mutagen also give him a +2 morale bonus to Constitution, but when he takes a penalty on Will saving throws and to Intelligence, he also takes a -1 penalty to Dexterity." replace="Poison immunity"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the ragechemist: ‹discovery/feral mutagen›, ‹discovery/grand mutagen›, ‹discovery/greater mutagen›, ‹discovery/infuse mutagen›, ‹discovery/tentacle›, and ‹discovery/vestigial arm›.

reanimator.name: Reanimator
description: ## Reanimator
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/20›

A reanimator is an alchemist who has discovered how to infuse a corpse with a semblance of life. Many work in tandem with necromancers to explore the fine border between the worlds of the living and the dead. A reanimator has the following class features.

::ab[Bomb]{l=1 icon=down alter=Bombs ability="A reanimator's bombs deal damage ‹misc/one die step› lower than normal (regular bombs deal d4s, ‹discovery/concussive bomb«s› deal 1d3s, and so on). This ability otherwise functions as and replaces the standard alchemist bomb class feature."}

::ab[Simple Reanimation]{l=7 icon=learn ability="A reanimator adds ‹spell/lesser animate dead› to his formula book as a 3rd-level extract. When he uses that extract, rather than drinking it, he injects it into the corpse he intends to animate, which rises as an undead creature under his control 1 hour later. The extract can only create ‹template/zombie«s› (including variant zombies)." replace="7th-level bomb damage increase"}

::ab[Create Undead]{l=13 icon=learn ability="A reanimator adds ‹spell/create undead› to his formula book as a 4th-level extract. When he uses that extract, rather than drinking it, he injects it into the corpse he intends to animate, which rises as an uncontrolled undead 1 hour later. The extract can only create corporeal undead." replace="13th-level bomb damage increase"}

::ab[Create Greater Undead]{l=15 icon=learn ability="A reanimator adds ‹spell/create greater undead› to his formula book as a 5th-level extract. This otherwise acts similarly to a *create undead* extract." replace="15th-level bomb damage increase"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the reanimator archetype: ‹discovery/alchemical zombie›, ‹discovery/elixir of life›, ‹discovery/eternal potion›, ‹discovery/mummification›, and ‹discovery/preserve organs›.

royal_alchemist.name: Royal Alchemist
description: ## Royal Alchemist
‹SOURCE Heroes of the High Court/11›

Paranoia runs rampant among the noble courts of the Inner Sea. On account of this distrust, many rulers often hire specialized alchemists capable of concocting protections from both poisons and diseases for the nobles and their guests. The royal alchemist tradition began in the nation of Rahadoum shortly after the Oath Wars as a replacement for the protection once offered by the clerics and priests of the region. Today, royal alchemists are found in the courts of Cheliax, Galt, Taldor, and, surprisingly, Geb, where living visitors to the Blood Lords are offered protection from the death around them.
::cskill{gain=diplo~kno lose=kna~sur}

::ab[Alchemical Antidote]{l=2 flavor="A royal alchemist can blend special mixes of antitoxin, medicine, and other remedies into alchemical antidotes that can be imbibed by anyone." order=npa icon=power-def info="The royal alchemist creates his alchemical antidotes when he prepares his extracts for the day; they remain potent for 1 day before losing their properties, and the cost of the materials used to make them is insignificant. An alchemical antidote does not qualify as an extract or an infusion for the purpose of feats or discoveries." passive="The royal alchemist has a pool of points equal to ‹misc/half› his alchemist level + his Intelligence bonus that he can divide among any number of alchemical antidotes, though the maximum number of points he can spend on a single antidote is equal to 1/2 his alchemist level (minimum 1). Each point grants the imbiber of the alchemical antidote a +1 alchemical bonus on saving throws against poison for a number of hours equal to the royal alchemist's Intelligence bonus (minimum 1)." ability="The royal alchemist can also create an antidote that protects against diseases. For every 2 points the royal alchemist spends from his pool, the imbiber of the alchemical antidote gains a +1 alchemical bonus on saving throws against disease for 24 hours. The royal alchemist can expend 10 points from his pool to provide immunity to diseases or poisons (decided by him at the time of the antidote's creation); this immunity lasts for a number of minutes equal to the royal alchemist's Intelligence bonus (minimum 1) after the antidote is imbibed. An alchemical remedy can protect against either diseases or poisons, but not both." imp6="The royal alchemist can alter the distribution of the bonuses of any unused alchemical antidotes as a @HLfull-round_action, either combining them or splitting the bonuses further. The royal alchemist must be holding all unused alchemical antidotes he wishes to alter in this way." replace="Poison use~swift poisoning"}

saboteur.name: Saboteur
description: ## Saboteur
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/36›

The saboteur is an alchemist who specializes in destroying the plans, materials, and allies of his enemies. A saboteur has focused his alchemical research toward new ways to conceal his presence, sow confusion, and blow up large structures.
::prereq{r=Gnome}
::cskill{gain=ke lose=kna}

::ab[Chameleon Mutagen (Su)]{icon=boost-down l=1 ability="A saboteur discovers how to create a special elixir that he can imbibe in order to heighten his ability to move undetected and his mobility at the cost of his physical might. When consumed, the elixir causes the saboteur's skin to change color to match the background and causes his hands and feet to secrete a sticky residue. This grants him a circumstance bonus on Stealth checks equal to half his alchemist level (minimum +1), as well as granting him a climb speed equal to half his base speed, for 10 minutes per saboteur level. In addition, while the chameleon extract is in effect, the saboteur takes a -2 penalty to his Strength." special="A saboteur who drinks an alchemist's mutagen is treated as a non-alchemist. All limitations to mutagens apply to chameleon mutagen as if it were the same ability. The ‹discovery/infuse mutagen› discovery and *persistent mutagen* class ability apply to the chameleon mutagen." replace=Mutagen}
::ab[Saboteur Discoveries]{icon=power head ability="The following four new options may be taken as discoveries by a saboteur."}

::ab[Bore Bomb]{sub icon=power ability="When the saboteur creates a bomb, he may choose to make it a bore bomb. If a bore bomb strikes a wall, gate, siege engine, or similar large, solid, inanimate structure, it ignores ‹misc/half› the target's hardness and deals 1 point of damage per saboteur level. If a bore bomb reduces an inanimate target to half its hit points or fewer, it blows a hole 5 feet wide and 5 feet deep in the target." special="This discovery cannot be used with other discoveries that modify bombs and have this same restriction (Primary Bomb Discoveries)."}

::ab[Complex Bomb]{sub icon=power ability="When the saboteur creates a bomb, he can choose to have it modified by two different discoveries that modify a bomb's damage (that normally cannot be used together). Each discovery modifies half the bomb's damage dice, rounding down. For example, a ‹discovery/concussive<_bomb›/‹discovery/frost<_bomb› bomb from a 9th-level saboteur deals 2d4 points of sonic damage + 2d6 points of frost damage." special="Creating a complex bomb counts as 2 daily uses of the bomb ability."}

::ab[Greater Chameleon Mutagen]{sub icon=boost ability="The saboteur's chameleon mutagen now also grants the saboteur the *hide in plain sight* ability, as the ‹class/ranger› ability of the same name, but it functions in any terrain." prereq="12th-level alchemist."}

::ab[Grand Chameleon Mutagen]{sub icon=boost ability="The saboteur's chameleon mutagen now grants him a circumstance bonus on Stealth checks equal to his alchemist level, as well as granting him a climb speed equal to his normal movement speed." prereq="16th-level alchemist and the *greater chameleon mutagen* discovery."}
::ab[Grand Discoveries]{icon=power head ability="A saboteur may select the following grand discovery."}

::ab[True Chameleon Mutagen]{sub icon=boost ability="The saboteur's chameleon mutagen now acts as if he was under the effects of the ‹spell/greater invisibility› and ‹spell/spider climb› spells, using his alchemist level as the caster level, for the duration of the mutagen." prereq="The *grand chamelon mutagen* discovery."}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the saboteur archetype: ‹discovery/acid bomb›, ‹discovery/concussive bomb›, ‹discovery/delayed bomb›, ‹discovery/explosive bomb›, ‹discovery/fast bombs›, ‹discovery/inferno bomb›, ‹discovery/madness bomb›, ‹discovery/precise bombs›, ‹discovery/smoke bomb›, ‹discovery/stink bomb›; ‹discovery/explosive missile›, ‹discovery/immolation bomb›, ‹discovery/siege bomb›; ‹discovery/blinding bomb›, ‹discovery/cognatogen›, ‹discovery/confusion bomb›, ‹discovery/tanglefoot bomb›.

sacrament_alchemist.name: Sacrament Alchemist
description: ## Sacrament Alchemist
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/11›

Sacrament alchemists serve in a great variety of priestly roles for their deities. A sacrament alchemist is often invaluable even to like-minded allies who are not adherents of the alchemist's religion but who nonetheless rely on his divine abilities.
::prereq{other="Like a cleric, a sacrament alchemist must worship a deity, and must be within one step of his deity's alignment."}

::ab[Sacramental Cognatogen (Su)]{icon=magic-down order=na info="A sacrament alchemist can anoint himself as his deity's servant using his own special version of a mutagen called a sacramental cognatogen. This is brewed, imbibed, maintained, and can nauseate non-alchemists in the same way as a mutagen. While a sacramental cognatogen is in effect, the sacrament alchemist takes a -2 penalty to Dexterity." ability="Instead of providing physical benefits, the sacramental cognatogen grants the sacrament alchemist access to the domain powers of one domain or subdomain belonging to his deity as if he were a ‹class/cleric› of his alchemist level. The sacrament alchemist must choose the domain or subdomain when he brews the sacramental cognatogen. A sacrament alchemist can select an alignment domain only if his alignment matches that domain. He treats his alchemist level as his cleric level for purposes of using these domain powers, and treats his Intelligence modifier as his Wisdom modifier for purposes of variable effects. The effects of the sacramental cognatogen last for 10 minutes per alchemist level." special="A sacrament alchemist doesn't gain the bonus spells listed for each domain, nor does he gain any bonus spell slots. Levels of classes that normally provide access to domains stack with the sacrament alchemist's alchemist level for purposes of determining domain powers and abilities, but not for purposes of determining bonus spells. Discoveries, feats, and effects that interact with the mutagen class feature do not affect sacramental cognatogen. A sacrament alchemist cannot take the *cognatogen* or *mutagen* discoveries." replace=Mutagen}

::ab[Divinely Inspired Alchemy (Su)]{icon=power l=3 flavor="A sacrament alchemist's faith inspires him with ideas for novel alchemical achievements, although the mental strain means that he can't reliably repeat them." ability="Once per day while preparing extracts, for a period of 24 hours, a sacrament alchemist can gain an alchemist discovery for which he qualifies. He treats his alchemist level as 2 lower for the purpose of this discovery." replace="Swift alchemy"}
@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the sacrament alchemist archetype: ‹discovery/combine extracts›, ‹discovery/dilution›, ‹discovery/infusion›, and ‹discovery/ranged baptism›.

tinkerer.name: Tinkerer
description: ## Tinkerer
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/46›

Tinkerers constantly dabble in clockworking, creating special familiars that they regularly upgrade and with which they form bizarre bonds. Many worship ‹faith/Brigh› and can be found throughout Golarion, especially in dwarven and gnome communities.

::ab[Clockwork Bond (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power info="A tinkerer forms a bond with one of her creations, and begins play with it at no cost. This functions as the ‹ability/familiar› option of the wizard's arcane bond class feature, with the tinkerer's effective wizard level equal to her alchemist level. If a tinkerer would gain a familiar through another class, those levels stack for purposes of determining the familiar's abilities." passive="A tinkerer's ‹sidekick/familiar› can take any shape the tinkerer wishes, but its statistics and abilities are identical to a ‹monster/clockwork spy› with the normal adjustments and abilities for a familiar applied, and it is a ‹type/construct› with the ‹subtype/clockwork› subtype. Unlike most familiars, a clockwork spy does not grant special abilities to its master." order=npa ability="With 1 minute of work and a successful DC 15 Heal check, a tinkerer can restore 1d4 hit points to her clockwork spy familiar." imp3="If the tinkerer has the ‹discovery/infusion› discovery, a clockwork spy familiar can deliver the tinkerer's extracts with a range of touch for her." imp4="The tinkerer can now restore 1d6 hit points to her familiar." imp7="A clockwork spy can communicate with constructs with the clockwork subtype." imp8="The tinkerer can now restore 2d6 hit points to her familiar." imp12="The tinkerer can now heal 3d6 hit points." imp16="The tinkerer can now heal 4d6 hit points." imp20="The tinkerer can now heal 5d6 hit points." replace=Mutagen special="A tinkerer cannot choose the *cognatogen* or *mutagen* discoveries."}

:::ab{l=2 title="Tinkering (Ex)" icon=boost-def-melee action=Ability imp5="The tinkerer can choose a second option from the list. The same option cannot be chosen twice." special="A tinkerer can change the enhancements applied to her familiar, but doing so takes 1 hour of work per enhancement she wishes to change." replace="Poison resistance +2~poison use~poison resistance +4"}
A tinkerer can spend 1 hour working on her clockwork spy familiar to enhance it with one of the options from the following list.
- The clockwork spy familiar gains DR 2/adamantine.
- The clockwork spy familiar gains resist cold 5.
- The clockwork spy familiar gains resist fire 5.
- The clockwork spy's slam attack deals 1d3 points of damage.
- The clockwork spy gains a +5 alchemical bonus on Stealth checks.
- The clockwork spy gains darkvision 120 feet.
- The clockwork spy gains a +2 resistance bonus on Reflex saving throws.
- The clockwork spy gains a +2 resistance bonus on Will saving throws.
- The clockwork spy gains a +2 natural armor bonus.
- The clockwork familiar's maneuverability is average.

::ab[Clockwork Familiar (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power ability="The tinkerer turns her familiar into a ‹monster/clockwork familiar› that takes any form she wishes. She must choose which type of item is installed in the clockwork familiar. She gains the clockwork familiar at no cost, as if she were a 7th-level spellcaster with the ‹feat/Improved Familiar› feat." replace="Swift poisoning"}
:::ab{l=8 title="Greater Tinkering (Ex)" icon=melee-def-power action=Ability replace="Poison resistance +6"}

A tinkerer can spend 1 hour working on her familiar to enhance it with one of the options from the following list. A tinkerer can change the enhancement applied to her familiar, but doing so takes 1 hour.
- The clockwork familiar's bite attack deals 1d4 points of damage.
- The clockwork familiar gains DR 10/adamantine.
- The clockwork familiar gains any feat for which it qualifies as a bonus feat.
- The clockwork familiar gains a +10 alchemical bonus on Stealth checks.

::ab[Clockwork Upgrade (Ex)]{l=10 icon=power order=am ability="A tinkerer has learned how to change the type of magic item her clockwork familiar can carry. This requires 1 hour of uninterrupted work. A tinkerer can change the type of item her clockwork familiar carries only once every 24 hours." move="A tinkerer can remove a spent item and install a new one." replace="Poison immunity"}

::ab[Clockwork Mimicries (Ex)]{l=14 icon=magic ability="A tinkerer can use her mechanical mastery to create clockwork mimicries of powerful magic items. Doing so takes 1 hour of uninterrupted work, and allows the tinkerer to create a ‹main/magic_>wondrous« item› that functions for a number of hours per day equal to her alchemist level. When creating such a clockwork item, the tinkerer must use materials worth an amount equal to the wondrous item's construction cost. She need not meet any of the item's other creation requirements. A tinkerer cannot create a mimicry of a consumable wondrous item or a wondrous item with finite charges, and she can have only one mimicry at a time. The clockwork item never functions as a wondrous item for any other creature." special="The tinkerer can spend 1 hour disassembling a clockwork mimicry and reassembling it into a mimicry of a different wondrous item. When she disassembles a clockwork mimicry, she can salvage parts worth ‹misc/half› of the mimicry's creation cost. She still must pay the full creation cost for the new mimicry. This resets the duration the mimicry functions per day." replace="Persistent mutagen"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the tinkerer archetype: ‹discovery/combine extracts›, ‹discovery/dilution›, ‹discovery/elixir of life›, ‹discovery/enhance potion›, ‹discovery/eternal potion›, ‹discovery/extend potion›, ‹discovery/infusion›, and ‹discovery/promethean disciple›.

toxicant.name: Toxicant
description: ## Toxicant
‹SOURCE Dirty Tactics Toolbox/7›

In lands such as Daggermark and Katapesh where poisons are legal and may be openly studied and sold, some alchemists obsess over the myriad ways that poisons and venoms can be applied and delivered. Known as toxicants, these deadly artists induce the production of deadly secretions within their own bodies in order to better inflict crippling conditions upon their foes.

::ab[Toxic Secretion (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power-def order=asw flavor="A toxicant has learned to mimic creatures with the ability to secrete harmful toxins through the skin." ability="Once per day, in a process that takes 10 minutes, the toxicant can create and imbibe a tincture that causes her skin to secrete a mild toxin. The toxicant is immune to this secretion, but any creature that hits her with a natural attack or unarmed strike must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the toxicant's alchemist level + the toxicant's Intelligence modifier). On a failed save, the target takes an amount of damage equal to the toxicant's Intelligence modifier. This is a poison effect." imp3="The toxicant can choose a condition to have her toxin impose. Once this choice is made, it can't be changed. A creature that fails its save against the toxic secretion also gains this condition until it succeeds at a save against the secretion, or until the toxin's duration ends. The initial list of conditions is: ‹rule/bleed› (1d6), ‹misc/dazzled›, ‹misc/fatigued›, or ‹misc/sickened›. At higher alchemist levels, the toxicant gains access to stronger conditions; some replace lower-level conditions, which must be selected first." imp4="A target that fails its save must succeed at a second save 1 round later or take the same amount of damage again. This effect repeats as long as the target continues to fail its saving throws, to a maximum number of rounds equal to 1 + 1 for every 4 alchemist levels the toxicant possesses (to a maximum of 6 rounds at 20th level)." imp6="The toxicant gains a second condition that her toxin imposes. She can choose from the previous list, or ‹misc/dazed› or ‹misc/shaken›." imp9="The toxicant chooses a third condition from the previous lists, or can replace *dazzled* with ‹misc/blinded›, or *dazed* with ‹misc/staggered›." imp12="The toxicant chooses a fourth choice from the previous lists, or can replace *fatigued* with ‹misc/exhausted›." imp15="The toxicant chooses a fifth choice from the previous options." imp18="The toxicant chooses a sixth choice from the previous options, or can replace *exhausted **and** staggered* with ‹misc/stunned›." standard="The toxicant can suppress this secretion, in which case it remains suppressed for 1 hour or until the toxicant reactivates it (also a @HLstandard_action)." swift="The toxicant can collect and concentrate this secretion into a poison she can deliver as a touch attack or apply to a weapon. Targets of such attacks must attempt saving throws as if they had touched the toxicant's toxic secretion. The toxicant can do this a number of times per day equal to her alchemist level + her Intelligence modifier." replace=Mutagen}

::ab[Toxic Digestion (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power standard="A toxicant can drink a poison and suspend it within her own body. She is not affected by the poison, and doesn't need to attempt saving throws against it. When the toxicant next uses her toxic secretion on a creature that attacks her with a natural or unarmed attack, she can expose the attacker to the digested poison (with that poison's normal duration, effects, and save DC) rather than her normal toxic secretion, even if the digested poison isn't normally a contact poison." special="The alchemist can maintain only a single digested poison at a time. If she digests a new poison, any older digested poison is lost." replace="Persistent mutagen"}

trap_breaker.name: Trap Breaker
description: ## Trap Breaker
‹SOURCE Dungeoneer's Handbook/23›

Trap breakers forgo the study of poison to instead master the construction and deployment of traps, using their nimble fingers to disable dangerous devices and their alchemical knowledge to create devastating land mines.

::ab[Trapfinding (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="A trap breaker adds ‹misc/half› his alchemist level on Perception checks made to locate traps and on Disable Device checks. A trap breaker can use Disable Device to disarm magic traps." replace="Poison use~poison resistance +2"}

::ab[Explosive Disarm (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power flavor="A trap breaker can attempt to disarm a mechanical trap by detonating it rather than disarming it with a Disable Device check." fullround="A trap breaker may expend one of his bombs to make a ranged touch attack against a trap, using the trap's Disable Device DC as its AC. If the attack misses, the bomb's splash damage activates the trap. If the attack hits, the bomb deactivates the trap without setting it off. The trap breaker must both know the location of the trap and be able to reach the trap's trigger with a ranged touch attack in order to use this ability." imp8="A trap breaker can disarm magic traps with this ability." replace="Poison resistance +4~poison resistance +6"}

::ab[Land Mine (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power flavor="A trap breaker can turn his bombs into land mines." provokes=Yes fullround="A trap breaker can set a land mine, which fills a single 5-foot square, and can't be placed in the same space as another trap, land mine, or magic trap. The trap breaker can arm the land mine with any bomb he is eligible to use, and the effects of this bomb are immediately transferred to the land mine." special="Creating a land mine uses up two of the alchemist's bombs per day. The DCs for Perception checks to notice the land mine, Disable Device checks to disable it, and saving throws to avoid its effects are equal to 10 + 1/2 the alchemist's level + his Intelligence modifier. Land mines last for 1 day per alchemist level or until they are triggered, whichever comes first. All alchemist land mines share the qualities listed in the trap block below." replace="Swift poisoning"}
::trap[Alchemist Land Minds]{mechanical cr=variable pdc="See above" dddc="See above" trigger=location eff="See above"}

::ab[Mine Engineering (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost flavor="A trap breaker's expertise in improving explosives allows him to create land mines with minimal expense." passive="Creating a land mine uses up only one of the alchemist's bombs per day." replace="Poison immunity"}

vaultbreaker.name: Vaultbreaker
description: ## Vaultbreaker
‹SOURCE Villain Codex/236›
The vaultbreaker combines stealth with his bomb-making talents to construct special focused charges that can disable or destroy hinges, locks, and similar mechanisms.
