::ab[Breaking and Entering (Ex)]{icon=skill-power passive="A vaultbreaker gains ‹skill/Stealth› (DEX) as a class skill and can disarm magical traps as if he were a ‹class/rogue› with the ‹ability/trapfinding› ability." replace="Brew Potion"}

::ab[Rogue Talents]{icon=power passive="A vaultbreaker can choose any of the following rogue talents in place of a discovery: ‹talent/expert leaper›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/ledge walker›, ‹talent/nimble climber›, or ‹talent/wall scramble›." alter=Discoveries}

::ab[Safecracking (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power flavor="A vaultbreaker's bombs can help him disable locks." ability="He gains ‹discovery/penetrating charge› as a bonus discovery." special="Because this ability replaces Throw Anything, the vaultbreaker's bombs do not add his Intelligence bonus to damage rolls." alter=Bombs replace="Throw Anything"}

::ab[Enhanced Safecracking (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost passive="A vaultbreaker gains ‹discovery/incendiary charge› as a bonus discovery, and his incendiary charges ignore 10 points of an object's hardness instead of 5." alter=Bombs replace="Poison use"}

vivisectionist.name: Vivisectionist
description: ## Vivisectionist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/20›

A vivisectionist studies bodies to better understand their function. Unlike a ‹arc-alchemist/chirurgeon›, a vivisectionist's goals are not related to healing, but rather to experimentation and knowledge that most people would consider evil. A vivisectionist has the following class features.

::ab[Sneak Attack]{l=1 icon=power ability="A vivisectionist gains the ‹ability/sneak attack› ability as a rogue of the same level. If a character already has sneak attack from another class, the levels from the classes that grant sneak attack stack to determine the effective rogue level for the sneak attack's extra damage dice (so an alchemist 1/rogue 1 has a +1d6 sneak attack like a 2nd-level rogue, an alchemist 2/rogue 1 has a +2d6 sneak attack like a 3rd-level rogue, and so on)." replace=Bomb}
::ab[Bleeding Attack]{icon=power ability="A vivisectionist may select the ‹talent/bleeding attack› rogue talent in place of a discovery."}
::ab[Torturer's Eye]{l=2 icon=learn passive="A vivisectionist adds ‹spell/deathwatch› to his formula book as a 1st-level extract."}
::ab[Cruel Anatomist]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A vivisectionist may use his Knowledge (nature) skill bonus in place of his Heal skill bonus."}

::ab[Torturous Transformation]{l=7 icon=learn ability="A vivisectionist adds ‹spell/anthropomorphic animal› to his formula book as a 2nd-level extract. When he uses this extract, he injects it into an animal as part of a 2-hour surgical procedure. By using multiple doses of this extract as part of the surgery, he multiplies the duration by the number of extracts used." imp9="A vivisectionist adds ‹spell/awaken› and ‹spell/baleful polymorph› to his formula book as 3rd-level extracts. When he uses the *awaken* or *baleful polymorph* extract, he injects it into the target (not a plant) as part of a 24-hour surgical procedure. He can make *anthropomorphic animal* ‹spell/permanen«t<cy› on a creature by spending 7,500 gp." imp15="A vivisectionist adds ‹spell/regenerate› to his formula book as a 5th-level extract."}
::ab[Crippling Strike]{icon=power l10="A vivisectionist may select the ‹talent/crippling strike› rogue talent in place of a discovery."}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the vivisectionist archetype: ‹discovery/alchemical simulacrum›, ‹discovery/concentrate poison›, ‹discovery/doppelganger simulacrum›, ‹discovery/feral mutagen›, ‹discovery/parasitic twin›, ‹discovery/preserve organs›, ‹discovery/tentacle›, ‹discovery/tumor familiar›, ‹discovery/vestigial arm›, and ‹discovery/wings›.

wasteland_blightbreaker.name: Wasteland Blightbreaker
description: ## Wasteland Blightbreaker
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/10›
The deleterious effects of the weird hazards of the Mana Wastes require special skill to remedy. Many wasteland blightbreakers are also ‹arc-alchemist/chirurgeon«s›.

::ab[Healing Infusion (Sp, Su)]{l=2 icon=power passive="The blightbreaker can prepare extracts of the ‹spelldef/healing› subschool as if he had the ‹discovery/infusion› discovery and can choose to render any such extracts inert and prepare other extracts to replace them (unlike infusions, which continue to occupy the alchemist's daily extract slots until consumed or used)." order=ps standard="If the blightbreaker has the infusion discovery, or if he is also a chirurgeon, he can use ‹spell/touch injection› as a spell-like ability once per day, but only to absorb an infusion of the healing subschool." replace="Poison resistance +2"}

::ab[Swift Healing Infusions (Sp)]{l=6 icon=aid standard="The blightbreaker can absorb an infusion of the healing subschool as if with *touch injection* an unlimited number of times per day." order=sw swift="While he has a healing extract absorbed in this fashion, he can deliver it." replace="Swift poisoning"}

::ab[Banish Blights (Su)]{l=8 icon=protect ability="A blightbreaker can end any effect affecting a creature that could be reversed by ‹spell/break enchantment›. To do so, the affected creature must imbibe one of the blightbreaker's infused extracts of the healing subschool of at least 3rd level. The blightbreaker must then attempt a caster level check as described in *break enchantment.* If the blightbreaker succeeds at the check, the creature is free of the spell, curse, or effect. The blightbreaker can also use this ability to attempt to dispel ‹rule/primal magic› events affecting a creature or an area. To do so, the affected creature must imbibe an infused extract as described above, and the blightbreaker must attempt a caster level check against a DC equal to 11 + the primal magic event's CR. If he succeeds at the check, the primal magic event ends as if the blightbreaker had successfully used dispel magic." special="If the blightbreaker attempts to dispel a primal magic event affecting an area, as a @HLstandard_action, he must dump an infused extract of the healing subschool of at least 3rd level onto an affected square. He then must attempt a caster level check against a DC equal to 11 + the primal magic event's CR. If he succeeds at the check, the primal magic event ends as if the blightbreaker had successfully used ‹spell/dispel magic›." replace="Poison resistance +6~the discovery gained at 8th level"}

@HL[Discoveries:] The following discoveries complement the wasteland blightbreaker archetype: ‹discovery/dispelling bomb›, ‹discovery/elixir of life›, ‹discovery/healing bomb›, ‹discovery/healing touch›, ‹discovery/infusion›, ‹discovery/spontaneous healing›.

winged_marauder.name: Winged Marauder
description: ## Winged Marauder
‹SOURCE Monster Codex/104›

Goblin alchemists have created a mixture of pheromones that they use to manipulate and entice giant vultures or dire bats to do their bidding. These goblins strap harnesses to the chests of these flying animals and hang beneath them, dropping flaming alchemical solutions on the helpless folks below.
::prereq{r=Goblin}
::cskill{gain=acro~ha~ride lose=dd~soh~umd}

::ab[Flying Beast Tamer (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A winged marauder acquires a flying mount as an ‹sidekick/animal companion›. This works as the druid ‹ability/animal companion› class feature, except the marauder must select a ‹companion/dire bat› or ‹companion/giant vulture›. The marauder's effective druid level is equal to his alchemist level." special="A marauder and his companion don't gain the *share spells* ability." replace="Mutagen~persistent mutagen"}
::ab[Discovery]{icon=power head passive="A winged marauder can select the following additional discovery."}

::ab[Sipping Pet (Ex)]{sub icon=aid standard="The marauder can administer a dose of a liquid (such as an extract, infusion, potion, or an alchemical liquid like ‹eq-misc/antitoxin›) to his animal companion, even if the companion is unconscious. The alchemist must be adjacent to or riding the companion to use this ability." prereq="The ‹discovery/infusion› discovery."}
